executive_brief: The Expense Tracker is a lightweight, single-user web application built with Next.js App Router. The system employs a client-side data pattern using localStorage for persistence, while utilizing a dedicated server directory for shared TypeScript types and validation logic. It is designed as a frontend-focused tool with future-ready route handlers to facilitate potential migration to server-side storage.

architectural_constraints: Stateless server-side logic (types and validation only in src/server)
Responsive UI requirements via Tailwind CSS
Mandatory unit testing for validators and component behavior before merge

maturity_assessment: The project requires REFINEMENT before full-scale execution. While the high-level structure and stack are well-defined, there is a critical lack of formal data schemas for the expense objects and missing API contracts for the proposed route handlers. The transition from a local-first approach to the 'future-ready' server architecture needs tighter specification to avoid integration debt.
critical_dependencies: Repository constitution compliance (Clean Code & Testing Discipline)
